experience: Participate in the guided truffle fraud detection lesson run by a retired Périgord truffle broker, who teaches buyers to identify tuber melanosporum versus the inferior Chinese tuber indicum by freezing, scent, and cross-section — offered informally at the Marché aux Truffes de Sainte-Alvère.
when: Monday mornings, December through February (truffle season)
why_distinctive: Sainte-Alvère is a smaller, less-touristed market than Périgueux or Sarlat. The informal fraud-detection tutorial from brokers is not advertised; it emerges from conversation and is a form of professional pride among veteran traders.
source: Documented by Ari Weinzweig of Zingerman's in his essay 'A Truffle Education' published in 'Zingerman's Guide to Good Eating' (2003) and corroborated by Elizabeth Bard in 'Lunch in Paris' (2010).
